1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL TO WITNESS those who dive and drag,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of those who harshly pull out the soul. (Of the disbeliever)

1.author: Arberry
1.text: By those that pluck out vehemently

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: By the angels who drag forth vehemently.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: By those (angels) who pull out (the souls of the disbelievers and the wicked) with great violence;

1.author: Maududi
1.text: By those (angels) that pluck out the soul from depths,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: By those who pull out, drowning.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: By those who drag forth to destruction,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: By those [angels] who wrest [the soul] violently,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: By the pluckers (the angels of death), violently plucking (the souls of the unbelievers),

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: By those [angels] who extract with violence

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: By the angels who violently tear-out the souls of the disbelievers from their bodies,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the angels who violently pull out the souls of the wicked,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: By [the winds] that pluck out vehemently

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: By the (angels) who tear out (the souls of the wicked) with violence;

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And those who undo the bonds gently,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And who softly release the soul. (Of the believer)

2.author: Arberry
2.text: and those that draw out violently,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: By the angels who release with gentle release.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: By those (angels) who gently take out (the souls of the believers);

2.author: Qarai
2.text: by those who draw [it] out gently,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: by the drawers (the angels of death), gently drawing (the souls of the believers),

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And [by] those who remove with ease

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: by the angels who gently release the souls of the believers,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And by those who gently draw out the souls of the blessed,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: By those who gently draw out (the souls of the blessed);

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: By the angels who glide swimmingiy,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by those that swim along (i.e. angels or planets in their orbits, etc.).

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and by those that speedily glide along (the cosmos),

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: by the angels who float (in the heavens by the will of God),

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And by those who float in space,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: and by [the clouds] that swim serenely and

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And by those who glide along (on errands of mercy),

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And then they speed with foremost speed.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And by those that press forward as in a race (i.e. the angels or stars or the horses, etc.).

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and vie with the others (in carrying out their Lord's behests);

4.author: Qarai
4.text: by those who, racing, take the lead,

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And those who race each other in a race

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: by the angels who hasten along

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Then those who are foremost going ahead,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: by those that outstrip them suddenly,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Then press forward as in a race,

5.author: Arberry
5.text: by those that direct an affair!

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And then they manage the affair,

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And by those angels who arrange to do the Commands of their Lord, (so verily, you disbelievers will be called to account).

5.author: Maududi
5.text: and then manage the affairs of the Universe (according to their Lord's commands).

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And by those who arrange affairs.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And those who govern the event,

5.author: Qarai
5.text: by those who direct the affairs [of creatures]:

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: and by the angels who regulate the affairs, (you will certainly be resurrected).

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Then those who regulate the affair.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: and by those who regulate events.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Then arrange to do (the Commands of their Lord),

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: The day the convulsive (first blast) shatters convulsively

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: On the day when the trembling one will tremble. (The disbelievers will certainly taste the punishment.)

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Upon the day when the first blast shivers

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: A Day shall come whereon the quaking will quake,

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: On the Day (when the first blowing of the Trumpet is blown), the earth and the mountains will shake violently (and everybody will die),

6.author: Itani
6.text: On the Day when the Quake quakes.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: The Day when the quaking will cause a violent convulsion,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: On the Day the Rajifah shakes,

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: On the day when the first trump resoundeth.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: the day when the Quaker quakes

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: On the Day, the shiverer shivers

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: On the Day the blast [of the Horn] will convulse [creation],

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: On the day when the first trumpet sound blasts

6.author: Shakir
6.text: The day on which the quaking one shall quake,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: On the Day when a violent convulsion will convulse [the world],

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: One Day everything that can be in commotion will be in violent commotion,

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And the following event will come after it.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and the second blast follows it,

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And there will follow it the next blast.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: The second blowing of the Trumpet follows it (and everybody will be raised up),

7.author: Itani
7.text: And is followed by the Successor.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and will be followed by another quaking.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: and is followed by the Successor,

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: There will follow it the subsequent [one].

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: and will be followed by the second one,

7.author: Shakir
7.text: What must happen afterwards shall follow it.

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Hearts will pound loudly on that day,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Many a heart will flutter on that day!

8.author: Arberry
8.text: hearts upon that day shall be athrob

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Hearts on that Day will be throbbing;

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: (Some) hearts that Day will shake with fear and anxiety.

8.author: Itani
8.text: Hearts on that Day will be pounding.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: On that Day some hearts shall tremble (with fright),

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: On that day hearts beat painfully

8.author: Qarai
8.text: hearts will be trembling on that day,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: hearts will throb on that Day

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Hearts on that day shall palpitate,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Hearts that Day will be in agitation;

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and their eyes shall be humbled.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and their eyes shall be downcast with dread.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: and their eyes will be humbled.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: and eyes will be humbly cast down.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Cast down will be (their owners') eyes.

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: They say: "Shall we go back to our original state

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: The disbelievers say, “Will we really return to our former state?”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: They shall say, 'What, are we being restored as we were before?

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: They say: shall we indeed be restored to the first state,

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: They say: "Shall we indeed be returned to (our) former state of life?

10.author: Itani
10.text: They say, “Are we to be restored to the original condition?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: They say: “Shall we indeed be restored to life,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: They say: "Shall we indeed be brought back from Al-Hafirah"

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: (Now) they are saying: Shall we really be restored to our first state

10.author: Qarai
10.text: They will say, ‘Are we being returned to our earlier state?

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: They say: 'What, are we being restored as we were before,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: They are [presently] saying, "Will we indeed be returned to [our] former state [of life]?

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: (The disbelievers) say, "Shall we be brought back to life again

10.author: Shakir
10.text: They say: Shall we indeed be restored to (our) first state?

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: They say, "What? shall we be brought back to life,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: They say (now): "What! shall we indeed be returned to (our) former state?

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: After having turned to carious bones?"

11.author: Arberry
11.text: What, when we are bones old and wasted?'

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: When we have become bones decayed!

11.author: Itani
11.text: When we have become hollow bones?”

11.author: Maududi
11.text: even after we have been reduced to bones, hollow and rotten?”

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Even after we are crumbled bones?

11.author: Qarai
11.text: What, even after we have been decayed bones?!’

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: even after we are fragmented bones'

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Even if we should be decayed bones?

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: after we have become bones and dust?"

11.author: Shakir
11.text: What! when we are rotten bones?

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: even after we have turned into decayed bones?"

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: "What! - when we shall have become rotten bones?"

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: They say: "Then this returning will be a dead loss."

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: They said, “So this return is an obvious loss!”

12.author: Arberry
12.text: They shall say, 'That then were a losing return!'

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: They say: that then shall be a losing return.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: They say: "It would in that case, be a return with loss!"

12.author: Itani
12.text: They say, “This is a losing proposition.”

12.author: Maududi
12.text: They say: “That will then be a return with a great loss!”

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: They say: "It would in that case, be a return with loss!"

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: They say: Then that would be a vain proceeding.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: They will say, ‘This is, then, a ruinous return!’

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: They will say: 'Then we are returned lost'

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: They say, "That, then, would be a losing return."

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: They have said, "Such a resurrection will certainly be a great loss".

12.author: Shakir
12.text: They said: That then would be a return occasioning loss.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: and they say, "That indeed would be a losing return."

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: They say: "It would, in that case, be a return with loss!"

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: It will only be a single blast,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: So that is not but a single shout.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: But it shall be only a single scare,

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: It will be only one scaring shout,

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: But only, it will be a single Zajrah [shout (i.e., the second blowing of the Trumpet)]. (See Verse 37:19).

13.author: Itani
13.text: But it will be only a single nudge.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: Surely they will need no more than a single stern blast,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: But it will be only a single Zajrah.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Surely it will need but one shout,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: Yet it will be only a single shout,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: But it will be only a single blow,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Indeed, it will be but one shout,

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: However, it will only take a single blast

13.author: Shakir
13.text: But it shall be only a single cry,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: But all it will take is a single blast,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: But verily, it will be but a single (Compelling) Cry,

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And they will wake up suddenly.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: So they will immediately be in an open plain.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And lo! they all shall appear on the surface.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: When, behold, they find themselves over the earth alive after their death,

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and lo, they will all be in the open plain.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: When behold, they are at As-Sahirah.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And lo! they will be awakened.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and behold, they will be awake.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: and they will be upon the surface of the earth (alive).

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And suddenly they will be [alert] upon the earth's surface.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: to bring them out of their graves and back to life on the earth's surface.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: When lo! they shall be wakeful.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and behold! They will all come out in the open.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: When, behold, they will be in the (full) awakening (to Judgment).

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Has the story of Moses come to you

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Did the news of Moosa reach you?

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Hast thou received the story of Moses?

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Hath there come Unto thee the story of Musa!

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Has there come to you the story of Musa (Moses)?

15.author: Itani
15.text: Has the story of Moses reached you?

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Has there come to you the story of Musa

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Hath there come unto thee the history of Moses?

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Did you receive the story of Moses,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Have you received the story of Moses?

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Has there reached you the story of Moses? -

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: (Muhammad), have you heard the story of Moses

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Has not there come to you the story of Musa?

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Have you heard the story of Moses?

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Has the story of Moses reached thee?

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: When his Lord called to him in Tuwa's holy vale?

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: When his Lord called him in the holy valley of Tuwa.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: When his Lord called to him in the holy valley, Towa:

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Recall what time his Lord called Unto him in the holy vale of Tuwa.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: When his Lord called him in the sacred valley of Tuwa,

16.author: Itani
16.text: When His Lord called out to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: When his Lord called him in the sacred valley of Tuwa,

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: When his Lord called him in the holy valley of Tuwa,

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: How his Lord called him in the holy vale of Tuwa,

16.author: Qarai
16.text: when his Lord called out to him in the holy valley of Tuwa?

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: His Lord called to him in the Sacred Valley of Towa,

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa,

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: when his Lord called him in the holy valley of Tuwa,

16.author: Shakir
16.text: When his Lord called upon him in the holy valley, twice,

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: His Lord called out to him by the sacred valley of Tuwa:

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Behold, thy Lord did call to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa:-

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Ith nadahu rabbuhu bialwadi almuqaddasi tuwan

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: "Go to the Pharaoh who has become refractory,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: That “Go to Firaun – he has rebelled.”

17.author: Arberry
17.text: 'Go to Pharaoh; he has waxed insolent.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Saying:'go thou Unto Fir'awn; verily he hath waxen exorbitant.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Go to Fir'aun (Pharaoh), verily, he has transgressed all bounds (in crimes, sins, polytheism, disbelief, etc.).

17.author: Maududi
17.text: and directed him: “Go to Pharaoh, he has rebelled,

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Go to Fir`awn; verily, he has transgressed all bounds.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: (Saying:) Go thou unto Pharaoh - Lo! he hath rebelled -

17.author: Qarai
17.text: [And said,] ‘Go to Pharaoh, for indeed he has rebelled,

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: saying 'Go to Pharaoh, he has become exceedingly insolent,

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: "Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: saying, "Go to the Pharaoh. He has transgressed beyond all bounds.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Go to Firon, surely he has become inordinate.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: [saying], "Go to Pharaoh, he has exceeded all bounds,

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: "Go thou to Pharaoh for he has indeed transgressed all bounds:

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: And say: 'Would you like to grow (in virtue)?

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: “Tell him ‘Do you have the inclination to become pure?’

18.author: Arberry
18.text: And say, "Hast thou the will to purify thyself;

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Then say thou: 'wouldst thou to be purified?

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And say to him: "Would you purify yourself (from the sin of disbelief by becoming a believer)",

18.author: Itani
18.text: And say, “Do you care to be cleansed?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: and say to him: 'Are you willing to be purified,

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And say to him: "Would you purify yourself"

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: And say (unto him): Hast thou (will) to grow (in grace)?

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And say to him, 'Would you [be willing to] purify yourself

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: And say to him, "Would you like to reform yourself?

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Then say: Have you (a desire) to purify yourself:

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: "And say to him, 'Wouldst thou that thou shouldst be purified (from sin)?-

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Faqul hal laka ila an tazakka

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Then I will guide you to your Lord that you may come to fear Him.'"

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: ‘And I may guide you to your Lord, so that you may fear.’”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: and that I should guide thee to thy Lord, then thou shalt fear?"'

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Then shall guide thee Unto thy Lord so that thou shalt fear.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And that I guide you to your Lord, so you should fear Him?

19.author: Itani
19.text: And I will guide you to your Lord, and you will turn reverent.”

19.author: Maududi
19.text: that I may direct you to your Lord and then you hold Him in awe?'”

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: "And that I guide you to your Lord, so you should fear Him"

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Then I will guide thee to thy Lord and thou shalt fear (Him).

19.author: Qarai
19.text: I will guide you to your Lord, that you may fear [Him]?’’ ’

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: that I might guide you to your Lord, so that you fear (Him)."

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And let me guide you to your Lord so you would fear [Him]?'"

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: I shall guide you to your Lord so that you may perhaps have fear of Him".

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And I will guide you to your Lord so that you should fear.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Do you want me to guide you to your Lord, so that you should fear Him?'"

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: "'And that I guide thee to thy Lord, so thou shouldst fear Him?'"

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: So he showed him the greater sign;

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: So Moosa showed him a magnificent sign.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: So he showed him the great sign,

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Then he shewed him the great sign.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Then [Musa (Moses)] showed him the great sign (miracles).

20.author: Itani
20.text: He showed him the Greatest Miracle.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Then Moses (went to Pharaoh and) showed him the Great Sign;

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Then he showed him the great sign.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And he showed him the tremendous token.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Then he showed him the greatest sign.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: He showed him the mighty sign,

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And he showed him the greatest sign,

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: Moses showed him the great miracle

20.author: Shakir
20.text: So he showed him the mighty sign.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Moses showed him the great sign,

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Then did (Moses) show him the Great Sign.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: In response, he denied and disobeyed.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: but he cried lies, and rebelled,

21.author: Maududi
21.text: but he denied it as false and disobeyed,

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: but the Pharaoh rejected it and disobeyed (Moses).

21.author: Shakir
21.text: But he rejected (the truth) and disobeyed.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: but he denied it and refused [the faith].

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: He then turned away, striving in his effort.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Then he turned his back, striving hard (against Allah).

22.author: Itani
22.text: Then turned his back, and tried.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: and then he turned back to have recourse to his craftiness,

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Then turned he away in haste,

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Then he turned back, walking swiftly,

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: Then he turned his back, striving.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Then he turned away in a hurry,

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: Then he quickly turned his back.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Further, he turned his back, striving hard (against Allah).

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: So he gathered the people, and proclaimed.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Then he gathered and cried aloud,

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Then he gathered his people and cried aloud,

23.author: Maududi
23.text: and gathered his people and declared:

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: So he gathered and called out,

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: then, he gathered, proclaimed, then said:

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And he gathered [his people] and called out

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Then he gathered (men) and called out.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: And he summoned all his people,

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Then he collected (his men) and made a proclamation,

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Saying: "I am alone your lord, the highest of them all."

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And then said, “I am your most supreme lord.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: and proclaimed, and he said, 'I am your Lord, the Most High!'

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And he said: I am Your Lord, most high.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Saying: "I am your lord, most high",

24.author: Itani
24.text: He said, “I am your Lord, the most high.”

24.author: Maududi
24.text: “I am the supreme lord of you all.”

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Saying: "I am your lord, most high."

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And proclaimed: "I (Pharaoh) am your Lord the Highest."

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: 'I am your lord, the most high'

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And said, "I am your most exalted lord."

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Then he said: I am your lord, the most high.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: and proclaimed, "I am your supreme Lord,"

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Saying, "I am your Lord, Most High".

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: So God seized him for the punishment of the Hereafter and this world.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Allah therefore seized him, in the punishment of this world and the Hereafter.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: So God seized him with the chastisement of the Last World and the First.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Wherefore Allah laid hold of him with the punishment of the Hereafter and of the present.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: So Allah, seized him with punishment for his last [i.e. his saying: "I am your lord, most high") (see Verse 79:24)] and first [(i.e. his saying, "O chiefs! I know not that you have a god other than I" (see Verse 28:38)] transgression.

25.author: Itani
25.text: So God seized him with an exemplary punishment, in the last and in the first.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Thereupon Allah seized him for the chastisement of the World to Come as well as of the present.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: So Allah seized him with punishing example for the Hereafter and the first (life).

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: So Allah seized him (and made him) an example for the after (life) and for the former.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: So Allah seized him with the punishment of this life and the Hereafter.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: So Allah seized him with the punishment of the Everlasting Life, and of this world.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: So Allah seized him in exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression].

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: So God struck him with the torment of this life and the life hereafter.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: So Allah seized him with the punishment of the hereafter and the former life.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: but God seized him and meted out to him the chastisement of both the next world and the present:

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: But Allah did punish him, (and made an) example of him, - in the Hereafter, as in this life.

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Truly there is a lesson in this for those who fear (the consequences).

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Indeed in this is a lesson for one who fears.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: Surely in that is a lesson for him who fears!

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Verily herein is lesson for him who feareth.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Verily, in this is an instructive admonition for whosoever fears Allah.

26.author: Itani
26.text: In this is a lesson for whoever fears.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Surely there is a great lesson in it for whoever would fear (Allah).

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: In this is a lesson for whoever fears.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Lo! herein is indeed a lesson for him who feareth.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: There is indeed a moral in that for someone who fears!

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Surely, in this there is a lesson for he that fears!

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Indeed in that is a warning for whoever would fear [Allah].

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: In this there is a lesson for those who have fear of God.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Most surely there is in this a lesson to him who fears.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: surely there is in this a lesson for the God-fearing.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Verily in this is an instructive warning for whosoever feareth (Allah).

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Inna fee thalika laAAibratan liman yakhsha

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Are you more difficult to create or the heavens? He built it,

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Do you think that it is harder to create you or the heavens? Allah has created it.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: What, are you stronger in constitution or the heaven He built?

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Are ye harder to create or the heaven which He hath builded?

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Are you more difficult to create, or is the heaven that He constructed?

27.author: Itani
27.text: Are you more difficult to create, or the heaven? He constructed it.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Is it harder to create you or the heaven? But Allah built it,

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Are you more difficult to create or is the heaven that He constructed

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Are ye the harder to create, or is the heaven that He built?

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Is your creation more prodigious or that of the heaven He has built?

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: What, are you harder to create than the heaven which He has built?

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Are you a more difficult creation or is the heaven? Allah constructed it.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: (People), is your creation harder for God than that of the heavens, which He created, raised and established

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Are you the harder to create or the heaven? He made it.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: [O Men!] Are you more difficult to create than the heaven which He has built,

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: What! Are ye the more difficult to create or the heaven (above)? (Allah) hath constructed it:

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Aantum ashaddu khalqan ami alssamao banaha

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: He raised its roof and made it proper.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: He lifted up its vault, and levelled it,

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: He hath raised the height thereof and perfected it.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: He raised its height, and He has equally ordered it,

28.author: Itani
28.text: He raised its masses, and proportioned it.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: and raised its vault high and proportioned it;

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: He raised its hei- ght, and has perfected it.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: He raised the height thereof and ordered it;

28.author: Qarai
28.text: He raised its vault and fashioned it,

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: He raised it high and leveled it,

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: He raised its ceiling and proportioned it.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: He raised high its height, then put it into a right good state.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: by raising its vault high and fashioning it flawlessly,

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: On high hath He raised its canopy, and He hath given it order and perfection.

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: Gave darkness to its night, and brightness to its day;

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And He made its night dark, and started its light.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: and darkened its night, and brought forth its forenoon;

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And He made dark its night, and brought forth its sunshine.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Its night He covers with darkness, and its forenoon He brings out (with light).

29.author: Itani
29.text: And He dimmed its night, and brought out its daylight.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: and covered its night with darkness and brought forth from it its day;

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Its night He covers and He brings out its forenoon.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: And He made dark the night thereof, and He brought forth the morn thereof.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: and darkened its night, and brought forth its forenoon.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: and darkened its night and brought forth its morning.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And He darkened its night and extracted its brightness.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: He has made its nights dark and its days bright.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And He made dark its night and brought out its light.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: and making its night dark and bringing forth its morning light,

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Its night doth He endow with darkness, and its splendour doth He bring out (with light).

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: And afterwards spread out the earth.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And after it spread out the earth.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: and the earth-after that He spread it out,

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And the earth!- thereafter He stretched it out.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And after that He spread the earth;

30.author: Itani
30.text: And the earth after that He spread.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: and thereafter spread out the earth,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And after that He spread the earth,

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: And after that He spread the earth,

30.author: Qarai
30.text: Thereafter He spread out the earth,

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: And the earth He extended after that;

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And after that He spread the earth.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: After this, He spread out the earth,

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And the earth, He expanded it after that.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: and the earth which He spread out,

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: And the earth, moreover, hath He extended (to a wide expanse);

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: He brought out its water and its pastures from it,

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And from it produced its water and its pasture.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: therefrom brought forth its waters and its pastures,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And he brought forth therefrom its water, and its pasture.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And brought forth therefrom its water and its pasture;

31.author: Itani
31.text: And from it, He produced its water and its pasture.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: and brought out of it its water and its pasture,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And brought forth therefrom its water and its pasture.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And produced therefrom the water thereof and the pasture thereof,

31.author: Qarai
31.text: brought forth from it its water and pastures,

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: and then brought from it its water and pastures.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: He extracted from it its water and its pasture,

31.author: Shakir
31.text: He brought forth from it its water and its pasturage.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: after that bringing forth from it its water and its pasture land,

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: He draweth out therefrom its moisture and its pasture;

32.author: Arberry
32.text: and the mountains He set firm,

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And the mountains! - He established them firm:

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And the mountains He has fixed firmly;

32.author: Maududi
32.text: and firmly fixed in it mountains;

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And the mountains He has fixed firmly,

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And He made fast the hills,

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: And the mountains He set firm

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And the mountains He set firmly

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And the mountains, He made them firm,

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And the mountains hath He firmly fixed;-

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: As convenience for you and your cattle.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: In order to benefit you and your cattle.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: an enjoyment for you and your flocks.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: A provision for you and your cattle.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: (To be) a provision and benefit for you and your cattle.

33.author: Itani
33.text: A source of enjoyment for you and for your animals.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: all this as provision for you and your cattle.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: As provision and benefit for you and your cattle.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: A provision for you and for your cattle.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: as a [place of] sustenance for you and your livestock.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: an enjoyment for you and your herds.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: As provision for you and your grazing livestock.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: All this was done as a means of enjoyment for you and your cattle.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: A provision for you and for your cattle.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: [all this] as a means of sustenance for you and your animals?

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: For use and convenience to you and your cattle.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: So when the greatest universal disaster arrives,

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Then, when the Great Catastrophe comes

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Then when the Grand Calamity shall come

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: But when there comes the greatest catastrophe (i.e. the Day of Recompense, etc.),

34.author: Itani
34.text: But when the Great Cataclysm arrives.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: But when the great calamity will come about

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: But when there comes the Greatest Catastrophe

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: But when the great disaster cometh,

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: But when the Great Catastrophe comes,

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: But when there comes the greatest Overwhelming Calamity -

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: On the day when the great calamity comes,

34.author: Shakir
34.text: But when the great predominating calamity comes;

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: When the great over-whelming event arrives,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Therefore, when there comes the great, overwhelming (Event),-

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: The day when man remembers all that he had done,

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: On that day man will recall all what he strove for.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: upon the day when man shall remember what he has striven,

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: The Day whereon man shall rember whatsoever he had striven for.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: The Day when man shall remember what he strove for,

35.author: Itani
35.text: A Day when man will remember what he has endeavored.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: on the Day when man will recall all his strivings,

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: The Day when man shall remember what he strove for.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: The day when man will call to mind his (whole) endeavour,

35.author: Qarai
35.text: —the day when man will remember his endeavours

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: the Day when the human will remember what he has worked for,

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: The Day when man will remember that for which he strove,

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: the human being will recall whatever he has done.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: The day on which man shall recollect what he strove after,

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: on the Day that man remembers what he strove for

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: The Day when man shall remember (all) that he strove for,

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: And Hell made visible to him who can see,

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And hell will be made visible to all those who can see.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: and Hell is advanced for whoever sees,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And the Scorch will be made apparent to any one who beholdeth.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And Hell-fire shall be made apparent in full view for (every) one who sees,

36.author: Itani
36.text: And Hell will be displayed to whoever sees.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: and Hell will be brought in sight for anyone to see:

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And Hell shall be made apparent for whoever sees.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And hell will stand forth visible to him who seeth,

36.author: Qarai
36.text: and hell is brought into view for those who can see—

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: and when Hell is advanced for whoever sees,

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And Hellfire will be exposed for [all] those who see -

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Hell fire will become visible for those who would see it.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And the hell shall be made manifest to him who sees

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: and Hell is there for all to see,

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: And Hell-Fire shall be placed in full view for (all) to see,-

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Then he who had been rebellious

37.author: Arberry
37.text: then as for him who was insolent

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Then as for him who waxed exorbitant,

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Then, for him who Tagha (transgressed all bounds, in disbelief, oppression and evil deeds of disobedience to Allah).

37.author: Itani
37.text: As for him who was defiant.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Then, as for him who rebelled

37.author: Qarai
37.text: as for him who has been rebellious

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: then as for whosoever was insolent

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: So as for he who transgressed

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Then as for him who is inordinate,

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Then, for such as had transgressed all bounds,

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: And who preferred the life of the world,

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And who chose the life of the world,

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And preferred the life of this world (by following his evil desires and lusts),

38.author: Itani
38.text: And preferred the life of this world.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: and preferred the life of this world,

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And preferred the life of this world,

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And chose the life of the world,

38.author: Qarai
38.text: and preferred the life of this world,

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And preferred the life of the world,

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And prefers the life of this world,

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: and prefers the life of this world,

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: And had preferred the life of this world,

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: Will surely have Hell for his abode.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: Then indeed hell only is his destination.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: surely Hell shall be the refuge.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: Verily the Scorch! that shall be his resort.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: Verily, his abode will be Hell-fire;

39.author: Maududi
39.text: most surely his abode shall be Hell.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: Verily, his abode will be the Hell;

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Lo! hell will be his home.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: his refuge will indeed be hell.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: surely, Hell will be their refuge.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: Then indeed, Hellfire will be [his] refuge.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: Then surely the hell, that is the abode.

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: But he who feared standing before his Lord, and restrained his self from vain desires,

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: And for one who feared to stand before his Lord and restrained his soul from desire,

40.author: Arberry
40.text: But as for him who feared the Station of his Lord and forbade the soul its caprice,

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: And as for him who dreaded standing before his Lord, and restrained his soul from lust,

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: But as for him who feared standing before his Lord, and restrained himself from impure evil desires, and lusts.

40.author: Itani
40.text: But as for him who feared the Standing of his Lord, and restrained the self from desires.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: But he who feared to stand before his Lord, and restrained himself from evil desires,

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: But as for him who feared standing before his Lord, and forbade himself from desire.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: But as for him who feared to stand before his Lord and restrained his soul from lust,

40.author: Qarai
40.text: But as for him who is awed to stand before his Lord and restrains his soul from [following] desires,

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: But, whosoever feared the standing before his Lord and prevented the self from desires,

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: But as for he who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from [unlawful] inclination,

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: However, those who had feared their Lord and restrained their souls from acting according to its desires.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And as for him who fears to stand in the presence of his Lord and forbids the soul from low desires,

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: but one who fears to stand before his Lord and restrained himself from base desires,

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: And for such as had entertained the fear of standing before their Lord's (tribunal) and had restrained (their) soul from lower desires,

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Waama man khafa maqama rabbihi wanaha alnnafsa AAani alhawa

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Will surely have Paradise for abode.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: Then indeed Paradise only is his destination.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: surely Paradise shall be the refuge.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: Verily the Garden! -that shall be his resort.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: Verily, Paradise will be his abode.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: most surely his abode shall be Paradise.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: Verily, Paradise will be his abode.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: Lo! the Garden will be his home.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: his refuge will indeed be paradise.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: indeed, their refuge shall be Paradise.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: Then indeed, Paradise will be [his] refuge.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: Then surely the garden-- that is the abode.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: Their abode will be the Garden.

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: They ask you: "When will the Hour be? When is its time fixed?"

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: They (the disbelievers) ask you regarding the Last Day, as to when is its appointed time.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: They will question thee concerning the Hour, when it shall berth.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: They question thee of the Hour:'when will its arrival be?

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: They ask you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) about the Hour, - when will be its appointed time?

42.author: Itani
42.text: They ask you about the Hour, “When will it take place?”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: They ask you about the Hour: “When will it be?”

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: They ask you about the Hour when will be its appointed time

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: They ask thee of the Hour: when will it come to port?

42.author: Qarai
42.text: They ask you concerning the Hour, “When will it set in,

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: They will question you about the Hour: 'When shall it be'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival?

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: (Muhammad), they ask you, "When will the Hour of Doom come?"

42.author: Shakir
42.text: They ask you about the hour, when it will come.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: They will ask you [Prophet] about the Hour, saying, "When it will come to pass?",

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: They ask thee about the Hour,-'When will be its appointed time?

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: What do you have to do with explaining it?

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: What concern do you have regarding its explanation? (You are not bound to tell them)

43.author: Arberry
43.text: What art thou about, to mention it?

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Wherein art thou concerred with the declaration thereof!

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: You have no knowledge to say anything about it,

43.author: Itani
43.text: You have no knowledge of it.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: What concern do you have to speak about that?

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: What do you have to mention of it.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Why (ask they)? What hast thou to tell thereof?

43.author: Qarai
43.text: considering your frequent mention of it?”

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: But how are you to know?

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: In what [position] are you that you should mention it?

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: (Muhammad), you do not know (when and how) it will come.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: About what! You are one to remind of it.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: what have you to do with the mentioning of it?

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Wherein art thou (concerned) with the declaration thereof?

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: The extent of its knowledge goes to your Lord.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: Towards your Lord only is its conclusion.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Unto thy Lord is the final end of it.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Unto thy Lord is the Knowledge of the limit fixed therefor.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: To your Lord belongs (the knowledge of) the term thereof?

44.author: Itani
44.text: To your Lord is its finality.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Its knowledge rests with your Lord.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: To your Lord it is limited.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Unto thy Lord belongeth (knowledge of) the term thereof.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Its outcome is with your Lord.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Its final end is for your Lord.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: To your Lord is its finality.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: This matter is in the hands of your Lord.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: To your Lord is the goal of it.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Your Lord alone knows when it will come;

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: With thy Lord in the Limit fixed therefor.

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Your duty is only to warn him who fears it.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: You are but a Herald of Warning, for one who fears it.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Thou art only the warner of him who fears it.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Thou art but a warner Unto him who feareth.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: You (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) are only a warner for those who fear it,

45.author: Itani
45.text: You are just a warner for whoever dreads it.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: You are only a warner to him who has a fear of it.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: You are only a warner for those who fear it,

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Thou art but a warner unto him who feareth it.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: You are only a warner for those who are afraid it.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: You are but a warner for those who fear it.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: You are only a warner for those who fear it.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: You are only a warner for those who fear such a day.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: You are only a warner to him who would fear it.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: you are but a warner for those who fear it.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Thou art but a Warner for such as fear it.

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: The day they see it, it will seem they had stayed in the world but only an evening or its turning into dawn.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: The day when they will see it, it will seem as if they had not stayed on earth except for an evening or its morning.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: It shall be as if; on the day they see it, they have but tarried for an evening, or its forenoon.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: On the Day whereon they behold it, it will appear to them as though they had not tarried save an evening or the morn thereof.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: The Day they see it, (it will be) as if they had not tarried (in this world) except an afternoon or a morning.

46.author: Itani
46.text: On the Day when they witness it—as though they only stayed an evening, or its morning.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: On the Day they see it, they will feel as though they had stayed (in the grave) no more than one evening or one morning.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: The Day they see it (it will be) as if they had not tarried (in this world) except an (`Ashiyyah) afternoon or its (Duha) morning.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: On the day when they behold it, it will be as if they had but tarried for an evening or the morn thereof.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: The day they see it, it shall be as if they had not stayed [in the world] except for an evening or forenoon.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: On the Day when they behold it, it will be as if they had lingered but an evening, or, a morning.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: It will be, on the Day they see it, as though they had not remained [in the world] except for an afternoon or a morning thereof.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: On the day when they see it, it will seem to them as though they had only lived in the world for a morning and an afternoon.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: On the day that they see it, it will be as though they had not tarried but the latter part of a day or the early part of it.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: On the Day when they see it, they will feel as if they had tarried in this world for only one evening or one morning.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: The Day they see it, (It will be) as if they had tarried but a single evening, or (at most till) the following morn!

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Kaannahum yawma yarawnaha lam yalbathoo illa AAashiyyatan aw duhaha
